name: confinement
summary: A PreToolUse fence that keeps a session scoped to os-rebuild: it blocks any tool call (Bash/Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob) that reads, writes, or cd's into a SIBLING project under ~/Projects (e.g. /Projects/harness, /Projects/OS), while leaving system paths and in-repo work untouched. Modeled on the harness Core's write-fence; the interpreter-level preventive tier.

admission.note: Candidate. Wired as PreToolUse in .claude/settings.json (a wired candidate is still a candidate). NOT admitted.

input.description: A Claude Code PreToolUse payload on stdin.

exit: Exits 2 (blocked) when a target resolves to a sibling project; 0 otherwise. Fail-open on any error.
invariants: A path inside ~/Projects but outside os-rebuild is a sibling project → blocked.
System paths (node, git, /usr, ~/.claude) and in-repo paths are allowed.
Fail-open: an unparseable/empty payload or any error never blocks (never wedges a session).
Interpreter-level (preventive); the bypass-proof guarantee is a kernel sandbox (the Core's claude-safe).
